title: Tender Japanese Mushroom Chawanmushi
description: A silky steamed egg custard enriched with earthy mushrooms and a crumbly toasted topping, comforting and satvik-friendly.
instructions: Combine dashi stock, sliced mushrooms, soy sauce, mirin, and sake in a saucepan and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the carrot julienne and simmer for 3 minutes until softened.
Remove the saucepan from heat and strain the liquid through a fine sieve into a heat‑proof bowl, discarding solids.
Whisk the eggs in a small bowl until smooth, then stir into the strained broth until fully incorporated.
Pour the custard mixture into a heat‑proof container, cover with foil, and steam over simmering water for 15 minutes.
While steaming, toast the bread slices until golden and crumble them into small pieces.
After steaming, remove the custard from the steamer and gently fold in the toasted bread crumbs and sesame seeds.
Serve the chawanmushi hot, garnishing with a few extra toasted crumbs if desired.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator and reheat gently before serving.
